description: A university space science team is adapting the 'janus_38mm' rocket at ~/Documents/rockets/janus_payload.ork to fly a fragile crystalline payload. Configure the rocket with a 38mm motor and flight settings to achieve an altitude of at least 1000m while ensuring maximum acceleration does not exceed 100 m/s² and launch rod velocity is at least 15 m/s. You may adjust the launch rod length up to a maximum of 3.0 meters and add a mass component to the nose cone to maintain at least 1.5 calibers of stability. Save the final configured design to ~/Documents/rockets/payload_ready.ork. Export an 'Acceleration vs. Time' plot of the successful flight to ~/Documents/exports/acceleration_plot.png and write a memo to ~/Documents/exports/payload_memo.txt documenting the chosen motor, the launch rod length used, the amount of nose weight added, and the final simulated altitude and acceleration.
